overview: Replace the Pulumi TypeScript infrastructure layer with Terraform HCL so the entire repo uses one language. The new terraform/ directory provisions the same Hetzner CX33 server with Tailscale zero-trust networking and triggers Ansible via local-exec, matching all current Pulumi behavior while incorporating review feedback: state security warnings, force_reprovision variable, --force-reauth in cloud-init, destroy-time Tailscale cleanup, and .gitignore updates.
goals: Single language (HCL) across the entire repo — infrastructure + workspace templates
Feature parity with current Pulumi provisioning: TLS key, firewall, server, cloud-init, Ansible trigger
Local state backend as default, with TF Cloud and Hetzner Object Storage documented as upgrades
Incorporate all accepted review feedback (state security, force_reprovision, --force-reauth, destroy-time cleanup, .gitignore)
Atomic swap — pulumi/ deleted and terraform/ created in a single commit
nonGoals: No Pulumi-to-Terraform state import — this is a clean cutover, documented as such
No S3/remote backend implementation — local state is the default, remote is documented only
No changes to Ansible roles, playbook, or provision.sh (except --force-reauth in cloud-init template)
No changes to Coder workspace templates (templates/base-dev, templates/docker-dev)
No SSH key name collision handling — single-server reference repo
successMetrics: cd terraform && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate passes
cd templates/base-dev && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate passes
cd templates/docker-dev && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate passes
Jinja2 templates (docker-compose, Caddyfile, daemon.json) render valid output
No Pulumi files remain in the repo
All config keys from Pulumi are present as Terraform variables with same defaults
rules: Local state is the default backend — no remote backend configured
terraform.tfstate contains secrets in plain text — must be documented prominently
.terraform.lock.hcl is committed to version control; terraform.tfstate is gitignored
All sensitive variables marked sensitive = true (suppresses CLI output but does not encrypt state)
provision.sh is unchanged — Terraform passes the same env vars as Pulumi did
Cloud-init uses --force-reauth for Tailscale to handle server replacement gracefully
Destroy-time provisioner attempts tailscale logout via SSH, fails gracefully if SSH is unavailable
force_reprovision variable allows re-running Ansible without server replacement
qualityGates: cd terraform && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate
cd templates/base-dev && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate
cd templates/docker-dev && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate

stories.title: Create terraform/ directory with versions.tf and variables.tf
stories.description: As a developer, I want the Terraform project scaffold with provider requirements and all input variables so subsequent stories can build on it.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/versions.tf exists with required_version >= 1.9
terraform/versions.tf declares providers: hcloud, tls, null (for terraform_data is built-in, no provider needed)

terraform/variables.tf declares all variables matching Pulumi config keys: server_name (default: coder-dev), server_type (default: cx33), server_location (default: fsn1), tailscale_auth_key (sensitive), coder_admin_email, claude_setup_token (sensitive), anthropic_api_key (sensitive), github_token (sensitive, default: empty string), force_reprovision (default: empty string)
All sensitive variables have sensitive = true
force_reprovision variable has description explaining its purpose for secret rotation
Example: tofu validate succeeds with just these two files (no resources yet)
Negative case: removing required_version causes validation to still work (it's a constraint, not a resource)

stories.title: Create firewall.tf with no-inbound Hetzner firewall
stories.description: As a developer, I want the Hetzner firewall resource that blocks all inbound traffic and allows all outbound, matching the current Pulumi firewall.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/firewall.tf creates hcloud_firewall named 'coder-no-inbound'
Three outbound rules: TCP 1-65535, UDP 1-65535, ICMP — all to 0.0.0.0/0 and ::/0
No inbound rules defined
Each rule has a description field matching current Pulumi descriptions
Example: tofu validate passes with firewall.tf included
Negative case: no inbound rules means no direction = 'in' blocks exist in the file

stories.title: Create user-data.sh.tftpl cloud-init template
stories.description: As a developer, I want the cloud-init template that bootstraps Tailscale on the server, matching current Pulumi user-data.ts behavior with the addition of --force-reauth.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/user-data.sh.tftpl is a templatefile()-compatible shell script
Template accepts two variables: tailscale_auth_key, hostname
Creates ubuntu user with sudo if not exists (matching current behavior)
Installs Tailscale via curl | sh
Writes auth key to /tmp/ts-authkey with mode 600, uses file: prefix
tailscale up uses --authkey, --hostname, --ssh, AND --force-reauth flags
Removes auth key file after tailscale up
Logs to /var/log/cloud-init-coder.log via tee (matching provision.sh shred target)
Shell variables use $${} escaping where needed to avoid HCL interpolation conflicts
Example: templatefile() renders with test values and produces valid bash
Negative case: no unescaped ${} that would be interpreted as HCL interpolation

stories.title: Create server.tf with Hetzner server and SSH key
stories.description: As a developer, I want the Hetzner server resource with SSH key, cloud-init user-data, and firewall attachment, matching current Pulumi server.ts behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/server.tf creates tls_private_key.deploy with algorithm ED25519
terraform/server.tf creates hcloud_ssh_key.deploy with name '${var.server_name}-deploy'

terraform/server.tf creates hcloud_server.coder with: name from var, server_type from var, location from var, image ubuntu-24.04, user_data via templatefile(), firewall_ids referencing hcloud_firewall.default, ssh_keys referencing hcloud_ssh_key.deploy
Example: tofu validate passes with all files so far
Negative case: no hardcoded values — server_type, location, name all come from variables

stories.title: Create main.tf with Ansible provisioner and destroy-time Tailscale cleanup
stories.description: As a developer, I want the terraform_data resource that triggers provision.sh with secrets as env vars, re-triggers on server replacement or force_reprovision change, and cleans up Tailscale on destroy.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/main.tf creates terraform_data.provision resource
triggers_replace includes both hcloud_server.coder.id and var.force_reprovision
provisioner local-exec runs bash ${path.module}/../scripts/provision.sh
environment block passes all secrets matching current Pulumi env vars: SERVER_NAME, SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, CODER_ADMIN_EMAIL, CLAUDE_SETUP_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN
A destroy-time provisioner (when = destroy) attempts SSH to the server and runs 'tailscale logout', with || true so it fails gracefully if SSH is unavailable
The destroy-time provisioner uses the server name from var.server_name for the SSH target
Example: tofu validate passes with all files
Negative case: destroy-time provisioner has || true — it must not block terraform destroy if SSH fails

stories.title: Create outputs.tf and terraform.tfvars.example
stories.description: As a developer, I want outputs matching current Pulumi exports and an example tfvars file with state security warnings, so users know how to configure and what risks exist.
acceptanceCriteria: terraform/outputs.tf exports: server_ipv4 (hcloud_server.coder.ipv4_address), hostname (var.server_name), deploy_public_key (tls_private_key.deploy.public_key_openssh, sensitive), provision_status (terraform_data.provision.id)
deploy_public_key output is marked sensitive = true
terraform/terraform.tfvars.example contains all variable keys with placeholder values

terraform.tfvars.example has a prominent comment block at the top warning about state file security: plain text secrets, recommend chmod 600, document Hetzner Object Storage and TF Cloud as encryption options
terraform.tfvars.example documents force_reprovision usage for secret rotation
Example: all quality gates pass with the complete terraform/ directory
Negative case: terraform.tfvars.example must NOT contain real secrets — only placeholders like <your-hetzner-api-token>

stories.title: Update .gitignore for terraform/ directory
stories.description: As a developer, I want .gitignore to exclude Terraform state files and tfvars from the new terraform/ directory while committing .terraform.lock.hcl, and remove Pulumi-specific entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove Pulumi section from .gitignore (pulumi/node_modules/, pulumi/bin/, pulumi/Pulumi.*.yaml entries)
Add terraform/ section: terraform/.terraform/, terraform/terraform.tfstate, terraform/terraform.tfstate.backup, terraform/terraform.tfvars, terraform/*.auto.tfvars
Do NOT gitignore terraform/.terraform.lock.hcl (it should be committed)
Existing templates/**/ patterns remain unchanged
Example: git status after creating terraform/ shows .terraform.lock.hcl as trackable but terraform.tfstate is ignored
Negative case: terraform.tfvars must be gitignored (contains real secrets) but terraform.tfvars.example must NOT be gitignored

stories.title: Delete pulumi/ directory
stories.description: As a developer, I want the Pulumi directory completely removed since it has been replaced by Terraform.
acceptanceCriteria: pulumi/ directory is deleted entirely: index.ts, server.ts, firewall.ts, user-data.ts, package.json, tsconfig.json, Pulumi.yaml, Pulumi.prod.yaml.template
pulumi/node_modules/ is deleted if it exists
No references to Pulumi remain in the codebase except in CLAUDE.md and README.md migration notes
Example: find . -path ./node_modules -prune -o -name '*.ts' -print returns no Pulumi TypeScript files
Negative case: templates/base-dev/main.tf and templates/docker-dev/main.tf are NOT deleted — those are Coder workspace templates, not Pulumi

stories.title: Update CLAUDE.md for Terraform
stories.description: As a developer, I want CLAUDE.md to reflect the new Terraform-based stack so future Claude sessions have accurate context.
acceptanceCriteria: Stack section: replace 'Pulumi (TypeScript)' with 'Terraform/OpenTofu (HCL)' for infrastructure provisioning
Reference section: remove openclaw-infra Pulumi file references (pulumi/index.ts, server.ts, firewall.ts, user-data.ts), keep Ansible and scripts references
Repo Structure: replace pulumi/ tree with terraform/ tree showing versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, server.tf, firewall.tf, outputs.tf, user-data.sh.tftpl, terraform.tfvars.example
Config Keys section: update to show terraform variable names (same names, different format — terraform.tfvars instead of pulumi config set)
Key Patterns section: update pattern 3 from 'Pulumi command.local.Command' to 'terraform_data with local-exec provisioner'
Key Patterns: update pattern 7 from 'Pulumi Cloud for state management' to 'Local state (terraform.tfstate) with optional remote backend'
Add new pattern: 'force_reprovision variable for secret rotation without server replacement'
Coding Style: replace 'TypeScript: strict mode, explicit types' with 'Terraform: HCL with consistent formatting, one resource per logical file'
Example: CLAUDE.md accurately describes current repo state
Negative case: no references to Pulumi remain in CLAUDE.md except optionally in a migration note

stories.title: Update README.md for Terraform
stories.description: As a developer, I want README.md to reflect the Terraform workflow so users can follow the Quick Start with the new tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Stack table: replace 'Pulumi (TypeScript)' with 'Terraform/OpenTofu (HCL)'
Quick Start section: replace pulumi commands with terraform equivalents: cd terraform, cp terraform.tfvars.example terraform.tfvars, edit terraform.tfvars, terraform init, terraform apply
Quick Start: remove 'cd pulumi && npm install' step
Quick Start: keep the ansible-galaxy collection install step

Add a 'State Security' section warning that terraform.tfstate contains secrets in plain text, recommending chmod 600, and documenting Hetzner Object Storage backend and TF Cloud free tier as encryption options
Add a 'Migration from Pulumi' note: if you deployed with Pulumi previously, run pulumi destroy first, then terraform apply (clean cutover, not import)
Add mention of force_reprovision for secret rotation in a 'Maintenance' or 'Operations' section
Example: a new user can follow Quick Start from clone to deploy
Negative case: no broken pulumi commands remain in README

stories.title: Update docs/setup.md for Terraform
stories.description: As a developer, I want the detailed setup guide updated to use Terraform commands instead of Pulumi.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 1 (Clone and Configure): replace cd pulumi && npm install with cd terraform, replace pulumi stack init with cp terraform.tfvars.example terraform.tfvars
Replace all pulumi config set commands with terraform.tfvars editing instructions
Section 3 (Deploy): replace pulumi up with terraform init && terraform apply
Section on what deployment does: remove 'Pulumi Cloud' reference, update to describe local state
Destroy section: replace pulumi destroy with terraform destroy
Add note about force_reprovision for re-running Ansible after secret rotation
Add state security note consistent with README
Example: following setup.md from scratch reaches a working deployment
Negative case: no pulumi commands remain in setup.md

stories.title: Run all quality gates and verify clean state
stories.description: As a developer, I want all quality gates to pass and the repo to be in a clean, consistent state after the migration.
acceptanceCriteria: cd terraform && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate passes
cd ansible && ansible-playbook playbook.yml --syntax-check -i inventory/hosts.ini.example passes
cd templates/base-dev && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate passes
cd templates/docker-dev && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate passes
Jinja2 template rendering test passes (docker-compose.yml.j2, Caddyfile.j2, daemon.json.j2)
git status shows no untracked pulumi files
grep -r 'pulumi' in code files returns no hits (docs may mention it in migration context only)
All changes committed and pushed
Example: fresh clone, cd terraform && tofu init -backend=false && tofu validate succeeds
Negative case: no .terraform/ directories or terraform.tfstate files are committed
